title: Chapter 1936 Seventh Heaven’s Dragon Spiral Mountain
content:
Chapter 1936 Seventh Heaven's Dragon Spiral Mountain

"When are you going to visit the Demon Sealing Grotto? I would love to go with you, but my position within the Demon Sealing Clan would only make things more difficult for you," Yan Hara said.

"I have some business at the Dragon Spiral Mountain, so I will be going there first. After that, I want to check up on my friends, but that won't take long. If nothing else comes up, I'll visit the Demon Sealing Grotto."

Yan Hara raised her eyebrows in a puzzled manner and inquired, "The Dragon Spiral Mountain? What business do you have with the Holy Dragon Clan? I thought they didn't accept visitors." "You're aware that my current body is only an avatar of my soul, right?"

"Yes, I am aware. The Celestial Emperor told us about you guys, but so far, you're the only person I've met who's from that world."

"Well, I intend on coming to the Nine Heavens with my real body, and the Dragon Spiral Mountain is the key to making that a reality."

"Coming here with your real body?! Such a thing is possible?" Yan Hara exclaimed in shock.

"The Dragon Spiral Mountain is actually a massive teleportation device that connects with the other Dragon Spiral Mountains, and one of those mountains is in my world. I'm not sure how it works, but I'll figure it out once I get there."

"That's only if the Holy Dragon Clan even allows you entrance," said Yan Hara.

Yuan pondered for a moment before asking, "At what level is the strongest individual there?"

"If you're thinking about forcing your way inside, forget about it. They have several God Ascension Realm powerhouses there," Sun Ling Cai said, shaking her head.

"Do you think I am a brute or something?" Yuan chuckled. "I just wanted to know their prowess."

Yan Hara then spoke, "If you're asking about their prowess, the Holy Dragon Clan is within the top 20 most powerful backgrounds in the Nine Heavens, and in terms of Dragon Clans, they're top 3."

"Top 20 in the entire Nine Heavens? That's quite impressive."

"If you need help, I can ask around," Sun Ling Cai said.

"No, it's fine. I have several plans in mind already."

"If you say so… but if you ever need my help, just ask. Here is my communication jade slip for contacting me." Sun Ling Cai placed her jade slip on the table before Yuan.

"Thanks."

Yuan accepted the jade slip.

Sometime later, after Yuan finished drinking all of the tea and they finished their conversation, Yan Hara spoke, "As much as I would like to travel with you, I don't want to bring unnecessary attention your way, especially from Qian Chu and his followers."

Yuan nodded. "I understand."

Sun Ling Cai then spoke, "Well, good luck with the Holy Dragon Clan. They're renowned for their stubbornness."

Sun Ling Cai and Yan Hara left first after paying for the tea.

"Let's head to the Dragon Spiral Mountain now, shall we?" Yuan said to Xi Meili, who eagerly nodded.

After leaving the tea house, they made their way to the nearest teleportation device.

Many teleports later, they arrived at Silverfoam Harbor, a bustling city located near the Sea of Dragons.

The Sea of Dragons was a vast, unique ocean in the Seventh Heaven, renowned for its abundance of sea beasts that bore the appearance of dragons. However, despite their fearsome looks, most of these beasts were not true dragons, and only a rare few possessed actual dragon blood in their veins.

No one truly knew why the sea beasts resembled dragons, but legend had it that a mighty ancient dragon once perished within those waters. Its blood seeped into the sea, tainting the depths and altering the creatures that dwelled within—giving rise to the dragon-like beasts that now roamed the Sea of Dragons.

As for the Dragon Spiral Mountain, it was located on a secluded island somewhere in the middle of the Sea of Dragons. Its remote and treacherous location was a natural deterrent to visitors, especially with powerful Immortal-level sea beasts prowling the surrounding waters.

While Yuan didn't fear the sea beasts, Xi Meili was a different story.

"Maybe I should stay in this place while you head to the Dragon Spiral Mountain. I don't want to be a burden," said Xi Meili as they stood before the Sea of Dragons.

Yuan shook his head.

"Don't say that. Also, your presence might help me convince the Holy Dragon Clan to assist us, so I cannot leave you behind. Just stay close to me. I'll protect you."

"Young Master, you're not alone."

Feng Yuxiang suddenly appeared before them.

"I'll also help out."

"Thank you…" Xi Meili nodded.

"We also have Yingzi, who is as powerful as a Golden Immortal," Yuan added.

Feng Yuxiang then spoke, "Instead of flying around like we usually do, it's about time we start making use of flying treasures."

With that, she retrieved a small crimson ship from her storage and tossed it into the air. The ship began expanding midair until it was large enough to accommodate a thousand people with ease. The sudden appearance of such a massive flying treasure left everyone at the harbor utterly dumbfounded.

"This is the Soaring Phoenix," Feng Yuxiang introduced the flying treasure to them as she jumped on it. "Although it's not the fastest flying treasure out there, it has what many ordinary flying treasures lack—defensive formations that will keep its riders safe. As long as we don't encounter any Immortal-level threat, we'll be safe while riding it."

Yuan and Xi Meili entered the ship a moment later.

"This is quite something. Why didn't you bring this out before? We could've used something like this during some of our long travels."

Feng Yuxiang then said, "Because it doesn't operate with just spiritual energy. It also requires Immortal Qi, which can only be found in the Seventh Heaven and above."

Moments later, the Soaring Phoenix stirred to life, and in the blink of an eye, it shot into the distance, vanishing from the harbor's view, leaving only ripples in its wake and stunned silence behind.
